# misp_user (Resource)

A MISP user. Creating a user may cause MISP to send a welcome email; API keys are not exposed here — manage them separately.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "misp_user" "analyst" {
  email    = "alice@example.com"
  org_id   = "1"
  role_id  = "3" # user
  disabled = false
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `email` (String) User email address. Must be unique within the instance.
- `org_id` (String) Owning organisation id.
- `role_id` (String) MISP role id (e.g. 1=admin, 3=user, 6=read-only). See `/roles` on your instance.

### Optional

- `autoalert` (Boolean) Receive automatic email alerts for new events.
- `certif_public` (String) PEM-encoded S/MIME certificate used to encrypt alerts.
- `change_pw` (Boolean) Require password change on next login.
- `contactalert` (Boolean) Receive contact requests via email.
- `disabled` (Boolean) Disable login for this user.
- `expiration` (String) Account expiry timestamp (RFC3339 or MISP date format).
- `gpgkey` (String) Armored GPG public key used to encrypt alerts.
- `termsaccepted` (Boolean) Whether the user has accepted MISP's terms.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Numeric MISP identifier.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
#!/bin/sh
# Import an existing MISP user by its numeric id.
terraform import misp_user.analyst 42
```
